L1.area: NAS message handling and mobility management procedures

L2.description: A null pointer dereference vulnerability exists in the processing of authentication-related NAS messages. When certain expected information elements are missing from incoming messages, the code accesses them without verifying their presence, causing the process to panic and crash. An unauthenticated attacker capable of sending crafted NAS messages can trigger this to cause denial of service for all connected subscribers.

L3.area: NAS GMM (GPRS Mobility Management) authentication message handlers
L3.description: A null pointer dereference vulnerability exists in the handling of NAS authentication response and authentication failure messages. When these messages arrive with missing information elements (IEs) such as authentication parameters, the code dereferences nil pointers while attempting to extract those parameters, causing a panic. An unauthenticated attacker can send crafted NAS messages to crash the service and disrupt all connected subscribers.

ground_truth.reason: The NAS authentication failure and authentication response handlers dereference IE parameters without nil checks, causing a panic when crafted messages with missing IEs are received.
